meaning: v.怒殴,打坏,<美俚>盛会,狂欢n.怒殴,猛击
examples: When you try to bash them, you usually end up punching yourself in the nose.
He gave Mike a bash on the nose.
I'm not sure I'll be any good but I'll have a bash.
CNN's Jim Acosta, Carol Cratty, Dana Bash, Ted Barrett and Alex Mooney contributed to this report.
The Bulldog Bash biker event in south Warwickshire has been cancelled this year, organisers have said.
